title: Zephyr coap_status.c handlers are dead code; Observe notify is a stub
description: Commit 49632f1115 ('/status + Observe') landed library-only code: coap_status.c exports const struct coap_resource lichen_coap_status_resource etc. (lines 710-724) but NOTHING registers them - no reference outside coap_status.c anywhere under lichen/. The file's own comment admits const resources 'cannot track observers', and lichen_coap_status_notify()/..._neighbors_notify() (lines 746-762) only LOG_DBG - they never call coap_resource_notify(), so Observe notifications can never fire. Contrast: coap_keys.c/coap_config.c use COAP_RESOURCE_DEFINE(..., lichen_coap, ...) which links them into the gateway service. Net: no app serves /status, /status/neighbors, /status/routes from this module; tests pass regardless. Fix: register the resources on the lichen_coap service (or delete the module), and wire notify to coap_resource_notify with proper observer tracking.
